word_list.word: it is all Lombard Street to a China orange
word_list.pronunciation: ɪt ɪz ˈɔːl lˈɑːmbɑːɹd stɹˈiːt tʊ ɐ tʃˈaɪnə ˈɔːɹɪndʒ

senses.definition: used to suggest that something is very certain or obvious, emphasizing the clear outcome or result of a situation

word_list.word: there is nowt so queer as folk
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ ɪz nˈaʊt sˌoʊ kwˈɪɹ æz fˈoʊk

senses.definition: used to suggest that people can be unpredictable and diverse in their behavior, emphasizing the importance of being accepting and respectful of individual differences

word_list.pronunciation: tɹˈuːθ fˈækt ɪz stɹˈeɪndʒɚ ðɐn fˈɪkʃən

senses.definition: used to suggest that real-life events can be more surprising and unusual than anything that could be invented, emphasizing that reality is often unpredictable and extraordinary
used to suggest that people who are seen as foolish or unimportant often have insights that others do not, because they have a unique perspective on the world or can notice what others cannot

word_list.word: blessed is he who expects nothing (, for he shall never be disappointed|)
word_list.pronunciation: blˈɛst ɪz hiː hˌuː ɛkspˈɛkts nˈʌθɪŋ fɔːɹ hiː ʃˌæl nˈɛvɚ biː dˌɪsɐpˈɔɪntᵻd

senses.definition: used to suggest that if someone does not expect too much from a situation or person, they will not be let down if things do not go as planned or hoped for

word_list.word: expect disappointment and you will never be disappointed
word_list.phonetic_text: ɪk.spɛkt dɪ.sə.pɔɪnt.mənt ænd ju wɪl nɛ.vər bi: dɪ.sə.pɔɪn.ɪd

senses.definition: used to suggest that managing expectations and anticipating potential setbacks can help people avoid disappointment and maintain a more positive outlook

word_list.pronunciation: ɛkspɪktˈeɪʃən ɪz fˈɑːɹ wˈeɪ bˈɛɾɚ ðɐn ɹˌiːəlaɪzˈeɪʃən

senses.definition: used to imply that the excitement or imagination of something can be more satisfying than the reality of it, suggesting that people should not build up their expectations too much
used to suggest that no matter how prepared someone is, there is always the possibility of unforeseen circumstances or events that can alter the outcome

word_list.word: what can you expect from a hog but a grunt

senses.definition: used to suggest that people should not have unrealistic expectations or demand too much from something or someone that is limited in its abilities or capacities

word_list.word: coming events cast their shadows before

senses.definition: used to suggest that future events can often be predicted by observing the signs or indications that precede them

used to suggest that circumstances and situations can change unexpectedly, making it unwise to make absolute statements or predictions about the future
